name: Adams County

actions.title: (City of Brighton) Meta data center campus approval
actions.summary: The City of Brighton approved Meta's plans for a large data center campus in the area, part of Meta's broader national expansion. The project brought discussions around water use and power infrastructure.

contextBlurb: Adams County, including the City of Brighton, has become a target for hyperscale data center development due to available land and proximity to Denver metro infrastructure. Water consumption in semi-arid Colorado is a key concern for local officials and residents.

name: Weld County

actions.title: Large-scale data center development near Platteville
actions.summary: Weld County approved site plans for data center facilities taking advantage of available land and energy infrastructure in the northern Colorado corridor. Discussions included grid capacity and economic development benefits.

contextBlurb: Weld County's open land and energy infrastructure (including proximity to natural gas resources) have made it attractive for data center developers. County officials have weighed economic benefits against infrastructure impacts.
